title: Undercut-vs-Overcut Mixture of Experts
summary: F1 strategy articles make clear that high-degradation/high-energy circuits favor the undercut, while low-degradation/slow warm-up tracks can favor the overcut; a single global classifier likely averages across opposite mechanisms.
rationale: F1 strategy articles make clear that high-degradation/high-energy circuits favor the undercut, while low-degradation/slow warm-up tracks can favor the overcut; a single global classifier likely averages across opposite mechanisms.
implementation_hint: Create a gating model from circuit, compound, degradation slope, lap-delta behavior, and field-relative pace proxies to estimate `undercut_favored` vs `overcut_favored`. Train separate experts per regime and blend them with leak-safe OOF predictions.
expected_effect: Should recover circuit-specific signal that a monolithic model smooths away, especially at strategy-extreme venues.
risk: Regime assignment may be noisy and the expert split can reduce effective sample size.
